title: plumeria rubra
intro: Plumeria rubra, also known as the red frangipani, is a tropical plant species that belongs to the Apocynaceae family and is native to the Americas. The plant is known for its fragrant, showy flowers that are often used in leis and other traditional Hawaiian floral arrangements, as well as its medicinal properties. The leaves and flowers of Plumeria rubra have been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, coughs, and digestive issues, due to their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. Additionally, herbal preparations such as teas and tinctures can be made from the plant, which are said to provide health benefits like reducing stress and promoting relaxation.
